If you’re someone who thinks beyond tickets, cares about architecture, and wants real ownership, this is for you.

What You’ll Gain
 • Opportunity to work on AI-driven platforms

 • The chance to build products used by real businesses at scale

- Comfortable with ambiguity and figuring things out from scratch

post.text:
Hi #Connections
We are hiring for Employee File Retrieval & Review Specialist

Role : Employee File Retrieval & Review Specialist
Salary : As per Market standard , Open for Negotiation and hike on current salary
Location : Taiwan ( Remote )
Experience : Minimum of 1-2 years of exp.
Contract : It is initial 5-6 Weeks of contract. 
Candidates must be living in Taiwan to apply for this role.

🌍 Overview
We are looking for detail-oriented professionals to support a global HR compliance project. This role focuses on locating, organizing, and reviewing historical employee files across multiple regions to ensure accuracy, completeness, and compliance.
If you enjoy investigative work, staying organized, and working independently—this opportunity is for you! 📂✨
🔎 Key Responsibilities
📁 File Retrieval – Locate and collect employee records from physical archives and digital systems.
🧾 Data Review – Assess files for accuracy and completeness based on project guidelines.
📊 Documentation – Track progress, maintain logs, and report findings to project leads.
🤝 Collaboration – Work with regional HR teams and procurement partners to resolve discrepancies.
🧠 Problem-Solving – Identify missing or hard-to-find documents and escalate issues when needed.
✅ Qualifications
✔️ Experience in HR administration, records management, or similar roles
✔️ Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
✔️ Ability to work independently and manage time effectively
✔️ Comfortable handling confidential information and data privacy standards 🔐
✔️ Familiarity with global HR processes is a plus 🌎
⚠️ Challenges & Expectations
📦 High Volume Work – Handling large quantities of files, some decades old
🏢 Physical Archives – Files may be stored in boxes, closets, or off-site locations
🕵️ Investigative Work – Records may be incomplete or misfiled
⏱️ Deadline-Driven – Efficient prioritization is essential
🌐 Global Coordination – Collaborate across countries and time zones
📌 Additional Details
🗓️ Duration: 5 -6 Weeks ( Candidates must be okay for 5 Week Short Term Project )
✈️ Travel: Occasional visits to storage locations may be required
💻 Tools: Microsoft Office & document management systems
👉 Interested candidates, please apply or DM directly or share your cv at npoddar@eteaminc.com for more details! 🚀

author.headline: --WORK FORM HOME

post.text:
🚀Hiring freshers can bring new energy, fresh perspectives, and long-term value to any organization.

While they may lack direct experience, fresh graduates often bring a strong academic foundation, a willingness to learn, and high adaptability. They are eager to prove themselves, quickly adjust to company culture, and contribute innovative ideas and enthusiasm to the workplace.

By hiring freshers, companies can shape and develop talent to meet specific business needs, building a workforce that is loyal and aligned with company goals and values.

Additionally, freshers are often cost-effective in terms of salary and tend to grow within the organization, helping reduce long-term recruitment costs. Investing in young talent fosters a diverse and dynamic team, keeping businesses ahead of industry trends through continuous learning and improvement.

Ultimately, hiring freshers is an excellent opportunity for organizations to invest in the future - creating a sustainable, motivated, and future-ready workforce that drives long-term success.

*Note:* Hindi Language is Mandatory.

author.name: Saurabh Aggarwal
author.headline: Technical Recruiter at CoverStack | Hiring Backend, Frontend & QA Talent | 10+ Years in Sales, Bulk & Tech Hiring | Talent Acquisition Specialist

post.text:
Rejections don’t slow us down. Silence doesn’t stop us. Delays don’t define us.

This week has been intense.

Multiple profiles shared.
Several interviews scheduled.
Some candidates cleared rounds.
Some got rejected.
Some are still waiting for final approvals.

That’s how real growth works — not perfect, but persistent.

In recruitment, you learn quickly:
✔ Every rejection is redirection.
✔ Every delay teaches patience.
✔ Every shortlist brings hope.
✔ Every offer changes someone’s life.

Success is not about one big closure.
It’s about showing up every single day — even when outcomes are uncertain.
Momentum is built in follow-ups.
Confidence is built in conversations.
Impact is built in consistency.

And we’re just getting started. 🚀

🔥 We are actively hiring!

If you’re a Backend Developer or Node.js Developer looking for a strong growth opportunity, let’s connect.

Your next role might be one conversation away.

#Hiring #NodeJS #BackendDeveloper #TechHiring #Opportunities #GrowthMindset #RecruitmentLife

author.name: Manjot Singh
author.headline: Building Agentic AI Products | Co-Founder | MVP in 30 Days | Turn Vibe-Coded Apps into Scalable Platforms | Pre-Vetted Teams in 7 Days

post.text:
We’ve been looking for a React developer for 4 months. Here’s why.

I hear this every week.

A startup reaches out: "We’ve posted on LinkedIn, Indeed, Naukri. We’ve gotten 300 applications. We can’t find anyone good."

The problem isn’t the talent pool. 

It’s the process.

Here’s what’s usually broken:

Problem 1: The JD is a wishlist, not a job.
"5+ years React, Next.js, TypeScript, Node.js, AWS, Docker, CI/CD, system design, leadership experience."
That’s 3 different people. Not one.

Problem 2: The assignment takes 8 hours.
Top developers have options. They won’t spend a weekend on a take-home for a company they’ve never heard of. 45-minute live problem-solving > 8-hour assignment.

Problem 3: Salary isn’t listed.
Developers scroll past job posts without salary ranges. Every. Single. Time.

Problem 4: No one is selling the role.
The best candidates aren’t applying to jobs. They’re being poached. You need to reach out to them with a reason to be interested.
Fix these 4 things and you’ll fill the role in 3-4 weeks, not 4 months.

This is what our recruitment team does at Technotribes. We don’t just post jobs , we fix the hiring process.

If your dev hiring pipeline is clogged, let’s audit it. 15 minutes, no pitch , just honest feedback. 

Link to book a call in my profile.
#Recruitment #HiringTips #TechHiring #TechnoTribes #StartupGrowth

We’re hiring SDE II(Backend) to join the team shaping the future of port operations worldwide.

Experience: 4-8 Years (SDE II backend)
Location: Hyderabad & Bangalore only
Notice Period: Immediate - 1 Month (Mandatory)

We’re building a next-generation, advanced Terminal Operating System — a platform that will redefine how global trade moves. From orchestrating cargo flows to optimising real-time operations, our new TOS is being engineered from the ground up with cutting-edge technology, modern architectures, and a product mindset that matches the world’s top tech companies.

We’re looking for builders who:

If you’ve built meaningful systems, if you love solving hard technical problems at global scale, and if you want your work to impact millions of containers, thousands of vessels, and the backbone of world trade — we want to meet you.

author.name: Kamal Sharma
author.headline: MERN Stack Developer & Trainer || Java Full Stack Developer & Trainer || Helping Developers Build & Think Better

post.text:
𝐌𝐨𝐬𝐭 𝐩𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐥𝐞 𝐟𝐚𝐢𝐥 𝐟𝐮𝐥𝐥 𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐜𝐤 𝐢𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐞𝐰𝐬 𝐧𝐨𝐭 𝐛𝐞𝐜𝐚𝐮𝐬𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐲 𝐜𝐚𝐧’𝐭 𝐜𝐨𝐝𝐞.

They fail because they don’t know what to revise.

Preparation is not about consuming more content.
It’s about revising the right concepts with clarity.

Most candidates:

Open random YouTube videos.
Revise advanced topics before fundamentals.
Prepare without structure.

And when interviews begin, clarity collapses.

𝑰𝒏𝒕𝒆𝒓𝒗𝒊𝒆𝒘𝒔 𝒅𝒐𝒏’𝒕 𝒕𝒆𝒔𝒕 𝒉𝒐𝒘 𝒎𝒖𝒄𝒉 𝒚𝒐𝒖 𝒌𝒏𝒐𝒘. 𝑻𝒉𝒆𝒚 𝒕𝒆𝒔𝒕 𝒉𝒐𝒘 𝒄𝒍𝒆𝒂𝒓𝒍𝒚 𝒚𝒐𝒖 𝒖𝒏𝒅𝒆𝒓𝒔𝒕𝒂𝒏𝒅 𝒇𝒖𝒏𝒅𝒂𝒎𝒆𝒏𝒕𝒂𝒍𝒔.

So I created something I wish I had earlier.

𝐅𝐮𝐥𝐥 𝐒𝐭𝐚𝐜𝐤 𝐃𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐥𝐨𝐩𝐞𝐫 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐞𝐰 𝐐𝐮𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 𝐌𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫 𝐏𝐚𝐜𝐤 (2026)

A structured, chapter-wise revision resource covering what actually gets asked.

It covers:

HTML – fundamentals to advanced
CSS – core concepts, performance, best practices
JavaScript – closures, event loop, async/await, prototypes
React – hooks, optimization, SSR vs CSR, error boundaries
Backend fundamentals – REST, APIs, middleware, JWT, CORS
Node.js and Express core concepts
SQL and MongoDB interview questions
Authentication and security essentials
System design basics
DevOps and deployment fundamentals
Project-based MERN preparation

HR questions to close confidently

Everything compiled into a single, structured PDF.

𝑵𝒐 𝒔𝒄𝒂𝒕𝒕𝒆𝒓𝒆𝒅 𝒑𝒓𝒆𝒑𝒂𝒓𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏. 𝑵𝒐 𝒈𝒖𝒆𝒔𝒔𝒘𝒐𝒓𝒌. 𝑱𝒖𝒔𝒕 𝒇𝒐𝒄𝒖𝒔𝒆𝒅 𝒓𝒆𝒗𝒊𝒔𝒊𝒐𝒏.

If you’re preparing for internships, placements, or developer roles in 2026, this will save you hours of confusion.

Comment below which section you want detailed notes on next:

JavaScript
React
Backend
System Design

Preparation without structure creates stress. Structured revision builds confidence.

Follow Kamal Sharma for more such content.

author.name: Samantha Osaduke, CHRP
author.headline: Human Resources Manager at The West Egg Group | Bilingual | The Peak’s 2025 Emerging Leaders’ List | Strong Relationship Builder

post.text:
Hiring Employed vs. Unemployed

I’ve been on both sides as a recruiter and candidate. 

Employed

- Confident and they don’t care as much, they’re more picky
- They apply to fewer jobs. Quality over quantity. To them, it’s a conversation
- They’re looking for a new challenge
- They will hold out for a better fit

Unemployed 

- More enthusiastic
- There’s a focus on volume and it’s a numbers game
- They’re more likely to move laterally or be more open to it

Both types are successful clinching offers. I have never automatically eliminated anyone for being unemployed and sometimes they’re the better hire. It often comes down to fit with the company and team. 
The pivoters aren’t starting over, but they’re taking their skills and knowledge with them to the new position. If you only hire from the traditional career paths, you’re missing out on talent and diverse ideas. Non-traditional hires are often adaptable and agile

author.name: MONISHA V
author.headline: Software Engineer | Frontend Development | React.js | Next.js | JavaScript | Web Applications

post.text:
🚀 Trending Fundamental Node.js Concepts Every Developer Should Master in 2026 🟢
Node.js isn’t just about building APIs anymore — it’s about understanding the core fundamentals that power scalable systems. Here are the concepts trending right now 👇
⚡ 1. Event Loop Deep Dive
Understanding how the Event Loop handles async operations is 🔑 to writing high-performance apps.
Microtasks vs Macrotasks? Promises vs setTimeout? Master this = master Node.
🧵 2. Async Patterns (Beyond async/await)
Callbacks → Promises → async/await → structured concurrency patterns.
Knowing when and why to use each makes your code production-ready.
🧠 3. Streams & Buffers
Handling large files? Real-time data?
Streams are memory-efficient and a MUST for scalable apps.
🛡️ 4. Security Fundamentals
Input validation, rate limiting, JWT handling, environment configs —
Secure coding in Node.js is becoming non-negotiable.
📦 5. Module System (ESM vs CommonJS)
The ecosystem is shifting toward ES Modules.
Understanding how imports/exports actually work under the hood is crucial.
🧩 6. Worker Threads & Clustering
Node.js is single-threaded… but not limited.
Leveraging worker threads & clustering helps unlock multi-core performance.
🌍 7. Observability & Performance Monitoring
Logging, profiling, memory leak detection —
Modern Node developers think beyond “it works” → they think “it scales.”
💬 Node.js is evolving fast, but strong fundamentals will always give you the edge.
Which core concept do you think most developers underestimate? 👇
#NodeJS #BackendDevelopment #JavaScript #FullStack #SoftwareEngineering #TechTrends

author.name: Shri Pranav
author.headline: Co-Founder & CTO @ HEPTA7 TECHNOLOGIES LLP | CTO @ Koothan Infotech | Technical Founder | Builder of Scalable Marketplaces & Software Products

post.text:
Handling 10,000 users is easy.
Handling 100,000 concurrent users is where your backend starts exposing your decisions.
Let’s talk about Node.js in real production.

First truth:
Node.js doesn’t magically scale because it’s “non-blocking.”
It scales when you design it correctly.
When we architect for 10K–100K active users, we don’t start with servers.
We start with failure patterns.

Here’s what actually matters.

✹ Event Loop Discipline
At 100K users, one blocking operation can freeze thousands of requests.
No synchronous crypto.
No blocking file I/O.
No heavy JSON parsing in request paths.
No CPU-bound loops inside controllers.
If you need CPU work — move it to:
– Worker threads
– Background queues
– Separate processing services
Node is excellent at I/O.
It is terrible at pretending to be a CPU engine.

✹ Stateless Architecture
If your Node app stores sessions in memory, you’ve already lost.
Horizontal scaling requires:
– Stateless APIs
– Redis for session/token caching
– Centralized auth validation
Because 100K users means multiple instances. Always.

✹ Database Becomes the Real Bottleneck
Node rarely dies first.
Your database does.
At scale, you need:
– Connection pooling discipline
– Read replicas
– Proper indexing strategy
– Query profiling before production
– Avoiding N+1 patterns
Most “Node performance issues” are actually bad SQL.

✹ Smart Caching Strategy
At 10K users, you can survive without caching.
At 100K, you must design cache layers:
– Response caching
– Query result caching
– Rate limit caching
– Idempotency key caching
Redis becomes a first-class citizen.
But remember: caching is consistency tradeoff engineering.

✹ Load Balancing & Process Scaling
One Node process ≠ scale.
Use:
– PM2 or cluster mode
– Container orchestration (Docker + Kubernetes)
– Proper health checks
– Graceful shutdown handling
Because killing a pod mid-request at 100K users creates silent chaos.

✹ Backpressure & Rate Limiting
You don’t scale by accepting everything.
You scale by controlling traffic.
– Rate limiting
– Circuit breakers
– Queue buffering
– Timeout discipline
Protect the system before protecting ego.

✹ Observability Is Non-Negotiable
Before reaching 100K users, you need:
– Structured logging
– Distributed tracing
– Metrics dashboards
– Memory leak detection
– Heap profiling

Node memory leaks at scale are subtle — and expensive.
Here’s the biggest mindset shift:
Scaling Node.js isn’t about increasing instance count.

It’s about removing single points of pressure.
CPU pressure.
DB pressure.
Memory pressure.
Network pressure.

At 100K users, architecture matters more than language.
Node.js can absolutely handle it.
But only if you engineer it like a system — not an app.
Curious — what broke first when your backend crossed 10K users?

post.text:
🚀 SigNoz is hiring Senior FrontEnd Engineers

We’re building the open-source observability platform developers actually want to use.

SigNoz is trusted by thousands of teams worldwide, with 26K+ GitHub stars and a rapidly growing developer community.

We’re looking for a top-tier frontend engineers to help push the product forward.

This role is high ownership, high velocity, and demanding.
It’s best suited for engineers who thrive in fast-moving startup environments and enjoy solving hard product and engineering problems.

What you’ll work on:
Observability products are some of the most complex frontend systems to build.

You’ll be working on:
• High-performance UIs handling large volumes of real-time telemetry data
• Complex data exploration workflows across logs, metrics, and traces
• Developer-first debugging experiences
• Performance optimizations where milliseconds matter

Frontend quality here matters deeply — speed, clarity, and usability are everything.

We’re looking for someone who:
⚡ Operates with high agency — You identify problems, propose solutions, and drive execution.
🚀 Moves fast — Comfortable shipping quickly and iterating in a high-velocity environment.
👨🏻‍💻 Has exceptional frontend fundamentals — Strong experience with React, Next.js, TypeScript, and modern frontend architecture.
🧠 Thinks like a product engineer — You care about the user and understand product tradeoffs.
🎯 Obsesses over UX and performance — You sweat the details and interactions.
🔥 Owns outcomes — Taking features from idea → production → iteration.

Why SigNoz:
• Open-source product with global adoption
• Real engineering challenges at scale
• High ownership culture — minimal process, no micromanagement
• Remote-first (India)
• Competitive salary + meaningful equity

You’ll work with a small team that moves fast and cares deeply about craft.

🔗 Apply Link in comment 

If you enjoy challenging work, high ownership, and building products developers love, we’d love to meet you

post.text:
I am hiring a part-time Project Manager and want to be clear about my expectations.

My firm runs tech projects for government and academic clients, and we are currently working on a disaster planning application for a US-based client in the central time zone. We need a PM who will take ownership of the process and drive execution independently.

Project details:
- Disaster planning app with heavy mapping capabilities
- Built on a React/Next.js stack, currently in the MVP stage
- Small team consisting of developers and a UX designer
- Heavy use of AI tools in the workflow

Role specifics:
- 20 hours/week, remote, US-based
- Run sprint planning, standups, and backlog refinement in JIRA
- Maintain a healthy backlog with well-written, prioritized tickets that include clear acceptance criteria
- Collaborate with UX on planning
- Prevent scope creep and provide clear status updates to the client
- Initial engagement of 3-6 months with the potential to extend
- Immediate start

Ideal candidate:
- A PM who takes ownership and requires minimal management

author.name: Subhasmita Chhotaray
author.headline: Associate - Procurement || Ashland || Ex Genpact Procurement & Vendor Management Professional

post.text:
Honest question to the hiring ecosystem:

Why are roles publicly posted if the hiring is already aligned through consultancies, referrals, or internal pipelines?

I’ve recently applied to multiple positions where my profile aligns very strongly with the JD. Yet, the applications were rejected without interaction.

I understand that hiring is complex. But repeated experiences like this can feel discouraging for professionals who genuinely match the requirement.

If the role is exploratory, pipeline-building, or already pre-aligned perhaps communicating that transparently would help manage expectations.

Candidates are not just applications in a system, we are professionals investing effort, time, and hope.

Would love to hear perspectives from recruiters and industry leaders.

author.name: Xiang Li
author.headline: Building the recruiting agent for startups

post.text:
Our agent told a customer that a candidate "doesn't have the must-have requirement of Next.js."

but the guys profile listed React and TypeScript. So yeah, he knows Next.js.

This is exactly the low-taste keyword matching our agent is built to avoid. We were confused.

Looked into it. The customer never actually spoke to our voice agent when he onboarded. He'd been copying entire profiles into ChatGPT and asking "is this person a good fit for my job description?"

So the calibration data our agent received was already pre-chewed by a generalized model. One that treats "React" and "Next.js" as unrelated keywords because it has zero taste about frontend frameworks and the relationships between them

Our domain-specific reasoning got overridden because the inputs were already slop before they reached us.

Still figuring out how to catch this. But if you're building a vertical AI product: the human in the calibration loop is load-bearing. Replace them (or let them replace themselves) with ChatGPT and your specialized agent outputs keyword matching with extra latency.

#hiring #startups #recruiting

author.name: Md. Mahfuzur Rahman
author.headline: Lead Programmer at Ascent Group

post.text:
Is PHP Really Losing Its Market to Next.js and Nest.js? 🤔
Recently, I’ve heard many people saying that with the rise of modern frameworks like Next.js and NestJS, PHP frameworks such as Laravel and CodeIgniter will gradually disappear from the market.
Personally, I don’t agree with that view.
Every technology has its own strengths and suitable use cases. PHP continues to be a powerful and practical choice for many types of projects. Here are a few reasons why:
1️⃣ Massive Market Share
 As of 2025, around 75% of websites worldwide still run on PHP in some form. This huge ecosystem means strong community support, countless libraries, and long-term sustainability.
2️⃣ Faster Development for Business Applications
 For startups and small-to-medium projects, Laravel is incredibly efficient. Features like database migrations, authentication, queues, and caching come built-in. Setting up similar capabilities in many modern JavaScript frameworks often requires additional configuration and tooling.
3️⃣ Cost-Effective and Easy to Maintain
 Studies and industry experience show that for many small and mid-sized projects, PHP-based solutions can reduce development costs by around 15% and shorten the maintenance cycle by nearly 20% compared to more complex stacks.
4️⃣ Mature Ecosystem
 With decades of evolution, PHP frameworks provide stability, mature tooling, and a massive developer base.
Of course, frameworks like Next.js and NestJS are excellent and very powerful, especially for modern full-stack JavaScript architectures. But that doesn’t mean PHP is going away anytime soon.
In reality, the industry is not about replacing technologies — it’s about choosing the right tool for the right problem.
What’s your opinion on this? Do you think PHP will remain strong in the coming years?
#PHP #Laravel #CodeIgniter #NextJS #NestJS #WebDevelopment #SoftwareEngineering

author.name: Prince Dwivedi
author.headline: Warehouse Manager | Inventory Management | Logistics | Supply Chain | Warehouse Operations

post.text:
I recently received a detailed job offer letter from a company called Matsbond Limited and NKC Builders, claiming to be in the construction, marine, engineering, and oil & gas sector.
 
 However, after doing thorough checks on LinkedIn, Google, and other platforms, I could not find any trace of the company’s existence — no website, no official registration, and no employee presence online. This raises serious doubts about the legitimacy of the offer.
 
 I’m sharing this to warn fellow professionals:
 ✅ Always verify company details before signing any contract.
 ✅ Be cautious if asked for upfront payments for visas, travel, or processing.
 ✅ Trust your instincts — if it feels unclear, question it.
 
 Stay safe, and let’s support each other in avoiding such traps.

 
#JobScamAlert #EmploymentFraud #StaySafeOnline #VerifyBeforeYouSign #ScamPrevention #FakeJobOffers #OnlineSafetyTips #JobSeekers #CareerAdvice #JobSearchTips #ProfessionalGrowth #LinkedInCommunity 
#WorkplaceWisdom #SupportEachOther #CommunityMatters 
#ShareToProtect #LinkedInAwareness #TogetherWeRise

author.name: Anirban Bhattacharya
author.headline: Assistant Manager- Corporate Relation at Anudip Foundation

post.text:
🚀 AI Hiring Trends in Kolkata – Insights from the Placement Desk
As a Placement Head closely working with AI-enabled ITES and emerging tech employers in Kolkata, I’ve observed a clear shift in shortlisting patterns.

Today, employers are not just hiring for “BPO” roles — they are hiring for AI-supported process roles with specific skill keywords.

🔎 The most searched keywords in recent JDs include:
• AI-Enabled Customer Support
• Data Annotation / AI Training
• Prompt Handling Basics
• CRM Automation (Salesforce / Zoho)
• Advanced Excel & MIS Reporting
• Power BI Fundamentals
• Cloud Support (AWS/Azure – L1)
• AML/KYC – FinTech Operations
• SaaS Support Executive
📌 Important Insight:
Candidates with properly aligned keyword profiles are getting shortlisted faster and accessing salary brackets of ₹15K–₹25K+ in entry-level tech-enabled roles.
As placement leaders, our responsibility is not just to place — but to prepare candidates for where the market is moving.
If you are a candidate, trainer, or employer looking to align with Kolkata’s AI hiring demand, let’s connect.
#AIHiring #KolkataJobs #PlacementStrategy #FutureSkills #Employability #WestBengal

post.text:
🚀 WE ARE HIRING 
🖥️ MERN STACK DEVELOPER 
📍 LOCATION : INDORE ( local candidate )
🎓 EXPERIENCE : 3+ years

📝 JOB DESCRIPTION : 

The candidate must have hands-on experience working with microservices, monolithic architectures, modular design patterns, and databases like MongoDB, PostgreSQL, and MySQL.

Additionally, experience with AWS, Docker, CI/CD, authentication mechanisms like OAuth and JWT, and WebSockets is required.

✨ RESPONSIBILITIES :
- Design, develop, and maintain scalable MERN stack applications using Node.js, Express.js, and Next js
- Build robust microservices and monolithic applications with a modular approach.
- Implement secure authentication mechanisms using OAuth, SSO, JWT, and role-based access control (RBAC).
- Develop highly responsive Next.js applications using RTK (Redux Toolkit) and UI libraries like Ant Design, Material UI, or Chakra UI.
- Manage databases including MongoDB, PostgreSQL, and MySQL for efficient data storage and retrieval.
- Deploy and maintain cloud-based applications using AWS services.
- Implement CI/CD pipelines for automated deployment and integration.
- Work with Docker to containerize applications and streamline deployments.
- Utilize WebSocket for real-time communication features.
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams using GitHub and Jira for version control and project tracking.
- Write clean, maintainable, and testable code following the best design patterns and software architecture principles.

 ✏️ Must Have Skills :
- Programming Languages : TypeScript, JavaScript.
- Backend Development : Node.js, Express.js, Next JS.
- Frontend Development : Next.js, React, Redux Toolkit (RTK), Any UI framework (Ant Design, Material UI, Chakra UI).
- Database Management : MongoDB, PostgreSQL, MySQL.
- Software Architecture : Microservices, Monolithic, Modular design.
- Version Control and Project Management : GitHub, Jira.
- Web Technologies : HTML, CSS, JavaScript.
- Core Concepts: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP); System Design (Low-Level & High-Level Design); Design Patterns (Singleton, Factory, Repository, MVC, etc.)

🌟 Good-to-Have Skills :
- Testing Frameworks : Jest, Cypress, Mocha.
- Monitoring and Observability : Prometheus, Grafana.
- Infrastructure Management : Cloud Formation, Kubernetes.
- Message Brokers : Kafka(Preferred), RabbitMQ

 shiva.official1964@gmail.com

author.name: Sharad Kumar
author.headline: React.js @PwC | Ex-Infosys/TCS | 2M+ Impressions | Helping Freshers

post.text:
🚀 EY React Interview Questions (Frontend Developer)
A candidate having 3+ years recently appeared for an interview at EY (Ernst & Young) and shared the key technical questions asked during the React round.

🛠 Tech Stack Focus: React JS

🔹 Core React Concepts
 1. What is useEffect?
 2. Custom Hooks – why & how to create them?
 3. Race condition in useEffect – how to prevent it?
 4. What are stale closures in React?
 5. Batching in React – how it works (especially in React 18)?
 6. Flushing in React – what is flushSync?
 7. Props drilling – what problems does it create?
 8. Lifting state up – when & why?
 9. Controlled vs Uncontrolled Components
10. How to prevent unnecessary re-rendering?

🔹 Advanced React (React 18 Focus)
11. Why does useEffect run twice in React 18 (Strict Mode)?
12. useMemo vs useCallback – differences & use cases
13. How do you call APIs in React? (Best practices)
14. What are Hooks? Why were they introduced?
15. State vs Props – core differences
16. useRef – practical use cases
17. useLayoutEffect vs useEffect
18. CSR vs SSR – differences & performance impact
19. useState vs useRef

🔹 Scenario-Based Question 
20. If there are 10 input fields, how would you:
Reduce API calls to just 1 request?
Prevent re-rendering for all 10 inputs?
Optimize performance?
(Think: Debouncing, Single state object, useRef, Memoization, Controlled inputs optimization)

Technical Round 2: Waiting

👉 Follow Sharad Kumar for daily doses of tech wisdom, corporate realities, and relatable IT life. 🚀

post.text:
We are Hiring For Full-Stack Developer (MERN + Python) At Gurugram - Hybrid

Company- Devkraft Technologies:
Job Title: Full-Stack Developer (MERN + Python)
Location: Gurugram ( Hybrid )
Experience: 3–6 Years

📝 Role Overview
We are looking for a versatile Full-Stack Developer who is an expert in the MERN stack but also possesses strong backend capabilities in Python. You will be responsible for building high-performance web applications, integrating AI/ML models via Python services, and ensuring seamless data flow between the frontend and a complex, multi-language backend.
 
🛠️ Key Responsibilities
MERN Development: Architect and maintain scalable web applications using MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, and Node.js.
Python Backend Logic: Design and develop high-performance microservices, data processing scripts, or AI/LLM integrations using FastAPI, Flask, or Django.
API Orchestration: Build and document robust RESTful or GraphQL APIs; manage communication between Node.js and Python-based services.
State & Data Management: Manage complex application states in React (Redux/Zustand) and optimize MongoDB schemas for performance and scalability.
Cloud & Deployment: Containerize applications using Docker and manage deployments on AWS/Azure via CI/CD pipelines.
 
✅ Technical Requirements
Frontend: Deep expertise in React.js, including Hooks, Functional Components, and performance optimization.
Node.js: Proficiency in building asynchronous, non-blocking APIs with Express.js.
Python: Strong command of Python 3.x, with experience in backend frameworks (FastAPI preferred) and library management.
Database: Advanced knowledge of NoSQL (MongoDB/Mongoose) and basic understanding of SQL (PostgreSQL).
Integration: Experience in bridging Node.js and Python environments (e.g., via message brokers like RabbitMQ/Kafka or gRPC).
Tools: Mastery of Git, Webpack/Vite, and testing frameworks like Jest or PyTe

Share your cv in DM or email at mehak.batra@devkraft.in

🚀 Hiring: MERN Stack Developer (2–5 Years) | Gravitones (Immediate Joining) 
If you list MongoDB, Express, React, Node on your CV 
you should actually understand how they work together.
We’re building AI-first products at Gravitones.
 Not tutorial clones. Not weekend hacks. Real systems used by real users.

MongoDB schema design (not just dumping JSON)
Express / Node backend architecture
React with proper state management & API integration
Async flows, authentication, performance basics
Debugging full-stack issues without panic

Startup pace is fast.
 Ownership is expected. 
 Excuses don’t scale.

post.text:
MERN – Full Stack Developer

How to Apply:
Please send your updated CV and portfolio (if available) to: jobs@excelbd.com
Subject Line: MERN Stack Developer – [Your Name]

Application Deadline: 25th March 2026

Job Context:
 
We are looking for skilled MERN Stack Developers with a strong focus on front-end development. You will build clean, responsive, and interactive web applications using React.js, supported by backend services developed with Node.js, Express.js, and MongoDB. If you have an eye for design and can transform mockups into seamless, user-friendly applications — we’d love to meet you.

Key Responsibilities:

• Develop high-performance, user-friendly, and responsive front-end interfaces using React.js
• Convert Figma/XD/Sketch mockups into reusable, pixel-perfect components
• Build and maintain scalable backend services using Node.js and Express.js
• Integrate RESTful APIs and handle asynchronous data operations using Axios/Fetch
• Collaborate closely with UI/UX designers, product managers, and fellow developers
• Ensure cross-browser compatibility, responsiveness, and adherence to WCAG accessibility standards

Requirements:
Education
• BSc in Computer Science or a relevant field OR Proven hands-on experience in front-end or MERN stack development

Experience
• At least 2 years
• The applicants should have experience in the following business area(s): IT Enabled Service

Additional Requirements:
• Age At least 22 years

Core Technical Skills (Must-Have)
• Expert-level knowledge of React.js, including Hooks, Context API, and component-based architecture
• Strong proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript (ES6+), and TypeScript & skilled in Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap, and other contemporary UI frameworks
• Deep understanding of responsive design, browser compatibility, and web accessibility (WCAG)
• Proven experience converting Figma/XD/Sketch designs into pixel-perfect components

Bonus Skills (Nice to Have)
• Experience with React Native for mobile applications
• Familiarity with animation libraries (e.g., Framer Motion, GSAP)
• Understanding of design systems and reusable UI component structures
• Knowledge of Docker or containerized development environments

What You’ll Need to Succeed
• 2+ years of professional experience in front-end or MERN stack development
• A solid portfolio of web applications or UI projects
• Strong attention to detail and passion for design consistency
• Ability to work independently and manage tasks with minimal supervision
• Excellent communication, problem-solving, and teamwork skills

Salary: Negotiable

Compensation & Other Benefits:
• T/A 
• Mobile bill
• Provident fund
• Weekly 2 holidays (Friday & Saturday)
• Lunch Facilities: Partially Subsidize
• Salary Review: Yearly
• Festival Bonus: 2

Workplace:
Work at office

Employment Status:
Full Time

Job Location:
Dhaka

#job #vacancy #hiring #jobpost #career

ComUnus is #hiring for MERN Full Stack Developer !! 😊
No Of Position : 4
Exp. Req : 3+ Yrs

Preferred Candidate : Mumbai
Kindly Note : Only Immediate Joiners are preferred !!

Interested Candidates Share there CV on : pradnya.bhalshankar@comunus.in | 8591400642

Job Summary:

We are looking for a skilled MERN Full Stack Developer with 3+ years of hands-on experience in developing scalable web applications using MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, and Node.js. The ideal candidate should be capable of handling both frontend and backend development, writing clean and efficient code, and delivering high-quality solutions.

Key Responsibilities

1. Develop and maintain web applications using the MERN stack (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js).
2. Design and develop RESTful APIs and backend services.

7. Perform debugging, testing, and troubleshooting of applications.
8. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including designers, product managers, and QA.
9. Participate in code reviews and follow best coding practices.
10. Deploy applications on cloud platforms and manage CI/CD pipelines.

Required Skills & Qualifications

1. Strong experience with MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, and Node.js
2. Proficiency in JavaScript (ES6+)
3. Experience with Redux / Context API
4. Knowledge of RESTful API development
5. Experience with Git version control
6. Understanding of authentication (JWT, OAuth)
7. Familiarity with HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap/Tailwind
8. Basic knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS/Azure) is a plus

author.name: Ali Mazhar
author.headline: Co-Founder| HR Consultant| HR Head| Recruitment Head

post.text:
Urgent Hiring!!
Job Title : Full Stack Engineer 
The Full Stack Engineer is responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining both the front-end and back-end components and platform. You will work on a wide range of technologies and systems, from the user-facing interface to the server-side infrastructure, ensuring seamless integration across global platform. You will be part of a cross-functional team that collaborates closely with Product, Design, and DevOps teams to deliver high-quality, scalable, and secure solutions that power global workforce and payroll services.

Responsibilities

You will develop high-quality, responsive web applications using TypeScript, Javascript, React, Express. Experience with NestJS, Vite, and Next.js is a plus.

You will design and implement server-side APIs, data models, and business logic using mainly Express framework

You will create client-side function-based React components, hooks, stories, and tests to interact with your server-side work

You will create and optimize database schemas, queries, and interactions with Postgres.

You will build reusable and modular components on both the front and back end that promote code reusability, maintainability, and scalability.

You will perform thorough testing, debugging, and troubleshooting of applications to ensure high-quality deliverables.

You will stay updated with emerging web development trends, frameworks, and best practices, and recommend improvements to enhance development processes.

You will participate in code reviews and provide constructive feedback to team members to maintain code quality standards.

You will communicate project progress, challenges, and solutions effectively to team members and stakeholders.

Qualifications

At least 3 to 4 years of Full-Stack experience, with a focus on TypeScript development.

Expert in the art of designing, coding, testing, and maintaining applications using the tech listed above.

Embrace independence and take ownership of your projects, yet also excel in a team setting and love to contribute.

Are passionate about solving customer problems with your coding superpowers, and are a natural at troubleshooting issues.

Take a business-focused approach to software development, with a keen eye on delivering high-value outcomes for our clients.

Obsess over details, and always keep the customer experience top of mind.

Extra brownie points if you:

Have experience with SaaS products running 24/7 on a major cloud vendor.

Solid understanding of RESTful APIs, microservices architecture, and asynchronous programming.

Experience with testing frameworks (Jest, Mocha, Chai, Storybook, React Testing Library) and automated testing methodologies (Cypress).

Knowledge of containerization technologies (Docker, Kubernetes)

Time Zone: EST
Apply : ali.mazhar@hyetelx.com

post.text:
Purplemerit is #Hiring for DevOps Engineer | Backend Developer | System Designer | Full-Time | 2–15 Years | Bengaluru (Onsite) or Remote (India)

At Purplemerit, we’re building systems that must be scalable, reliable, and performance-driven and we’re looking for engineers who genuinely care about craftsmanship.

If you’re passionate about solving real engineering problems, taking ownership, and growing into bigger responsibilities, this is for you.
 Only serious, high-ownership candidates should apply.

Open Full-Time Roles (2–15 years)
1) DevOps Engineer 
Help us build rock-solid infrastructure and deployment systems that keep production stable.
 You’ll work on:
CI/CD pipelines (automated build-test-deploy, release quality, rollback strategies)
Cloud + environments (prod/stage/dev hygiene, access control, secrets)
Containerization & orchestration (Docker/Kubernetes-style deployments)
Monitoring, logging, alerting (visibility, incident response, reliability improvements)
Automation to reduce manual ops and improve engineering velocity
✅ Apply for DevOps Engineer: https://lnkd.in/d-Dzr9JB

2) Backend Developer — Java / MERN 
Build backend services that are clean, secure, and ready to scale.
 You’ll work on:
Designing and building APIs and backend services (REST, integrations)
Database design, performance optimization, caching, and background jobs
Improving latency, stability, security, and code maintainability
Debugging production issues and building systems that don’t break under load
Working closely with product/engineering to ship end-to-end features
Tech: Java and/or MERN (Node.js, Express, MongoDB) based on team fit.
✅ Apply for Backend Developer (Java / MERN): https://lnkd.in/dhjR5yme

3) System Designer / System Design Engineer 
Own architecture thinking and help shape how our systems evolve as we scale.
 You’ll work on:
HLD/LLD, architecture decisions, and long-term maintainability
Designing for scalability, availability, observability, and cost efficiency
Distributed systems concepts: microservices, queues, caching, DB tradeoffs
Identifying bottlenecks and improving platform-level performance
✅ Apply for System Designer: https://lnkd.in/dXEM2EfE

Work Mode & Location
Remote (India) or Onsite in Bengaluru (Candidates can choose)
Full-time roles with real ownership and growth scope

Shortlisted candidates will be contacted with in 24-48 hours please check your mails

author.name: Shashank Goyal
author.headline: BCA Graduate | Technical Consultant Intern (Oracle HCM) | MERN Stack • SQL • Java | Open to Work

post.text:
🤖 AI is NOT replacing you. But someone who KNOWS AI will.
Here's what's happening RIGHT NOW in March 2026 👇
📌 Oracle is cutting thousands of jobs — specifically roles they expect AI to handle. Glassdoor

📌 In 2025 alone, companies cited AI in 55,000+ layoffs — 12x more than just 2 years ago. College Vidya

📌 But here's the flip side — AI is reshaping jobs, not erasing them. Companies are hiring people who can WORK WITH AI, not against it. Neevedu

📌 Experts predict new roles in AI governance, transparency, safety & data management are booming in 2026. Learning Routes

💡 So what should YOU do as a fresher/developer?
✔️ Learn Prompt Engineering 
✔️ Add AI tools to your tech stack (ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude) 
✔️ Build projects that USE AI — not just about AI 
✔️ Upskill in cybersecurity, cloud & data — these are AI-proof skills
🎯 I'm a BCA graduate from Jaipur—actively building my skills in MERN Stack, Oracle HCM & Cybersecurity.
The world is changing fast. The question is — are you adapting?
Drop a 🔥 if you agree!
#AI #TechJobs #FreshersIndia #MERN #OracleHCM #ArtificialIntelligence #2026 #Jaipur #CareerGrowth #Developer

🚀 We are hiring for the role of AVP – Client Services (MERN Stack). We are looking for an experienced technology leader with strong expertise in MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, and Node.js who can drive scalable solution architecture, lead development teams, and work closely with global clients to deliver high-impact technology solutions.

Experience: 10+ Years
Job Location: Remote/ Hybrid
Qualifications: B Tech / M Tech/ MCA or Higher
Work Timings: 1:30 PM to 10:30 PM IST
Functional Area: Architecture, Development and Technology Management
Title: AVP Client Services
We are seeking an innovative and experienced technology leader with expertise in the MERN stack (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js) to join our dynamic and rapidly growing development team. As an Associate vice president you will play a pivotal role in designing, developing, and delivering scalable solutions that address complex business challenges while driving technical excellence and mentoring high-performing teams.
Key Responsibilities
Engage with clients to understand their business objectives, translating them into actionable technical requirements.
Responsible to lead end-to-end project delivery, proactively addressing technical challenges and driving accountability at all stages of development.
Manage, mentor, and guide development teams to achieve project goals.
Define, implement, and enforce technical best practices to ensure high-quality, efficient processes.
Troubleshoot complex technical issues to enhance system performance and reliability.
Drive initiatives to optimize software development life cycles using DevOps practices, CI/CD pipelines, and automation.
Define and drive the long-term technical roadmap for the MEAN stack and related technology solutions.
Skills, Knowledge, and Experience
10+ years of software development experience, with expertise in MERN (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js) stack.
Strong hands-on experience in microservices architecture, design patterns, and containerization technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes (mandatory).
Proficient in both relational (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL) and non-relational databases.
Hands on experience with AWS services such as Glue, S3, Athena, Data Pipeline, and Lambda is a big plus.
Experience with DevOps tools such as AWS Code Pipeline, CI/CD pipelines, and automation frameworks is a plus.
Experience in industries such as Healthcare, Life Sciences, Supply Chain, or Manufacturing is a strong advantage.
Proven experience managing teams, delivering projects, and driving technical excellence.

💡 𝐌𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐚𝐲 𝐓𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡𝐭:
 𝐸𝑣𝑒𝑟𝑦𝑜𝑛𝑒 𝑡𝑎𝑙𝑘𝑠 𝑎𝑏𝑜𝑢𝑡 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝐴𝐼 ℎ𝑖𝑟𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑏𝑜𝑜𝑚. 𝐵𝑢𝑡 𝑠𝑜𝑚𝑒𝑡ℎ𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑖𝑛𝑡𝑒𝑟𝑒𝑠𝑡𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑖𝑠 ℎ𝑎𝑝𝑝𝑒𝑛𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑖𝑛 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝐸𝑢𝑟𝑜𝑝𝑒𝑎𝑛 𝑡𝑒𝑐ℎ 𝑚𝑎𝑟𝑘𝑒𝑡.

Many companies are posting more roles but hiring fewer people.
W𝐡y?

Because roles are increasingly designed for hybrid skill professionals.
Example trends:
 • DevOps + Security
 • Data Engineer + AI/ML exposure
 • Cloud Engineer + FinOps knowledge
 • QA + Automation + AI testing tools

𝗜𝗻 𝗼𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗿 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗱𝘀 - 𝘁𝗵𝗲 “𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗴𝗹𝗲 𝘀𝗸𝗶𝗹𝗹 𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹𝗶𝘀𝘁” 𝗲𝗿𝗮 𝗶𝘀 𝗳𝗮𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴.

📌 The real question for tech professionals today:

𝐴𝑟𝑒 𝑦𝑜𝑢 𝑔𝑟𝑜𝑤𝑖𝑛𝑔 ℎ𝑜𝑟𝑖𝑧𝑜𝑛𝑡𝑎𝑙𝑙𝑦 𝑜𝑟 𝑜𝑛𝑙𝑦 𝑣𝑒𝑟𝑡𝑖𝑐𝑎𝑙𝑙𝑦?
𝐵𝑒𝑐𝑎𝑢𝑠𝑒 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑚𝑎𝑟𝑘𝑒𝑡 𝑠𝑒𝑒𝑚𝑠 𝑡𝑜 𝑟𝑒𝑤𝑎𝑟𝑑 𝑠𝑘𝑖𝑙𝑙 𝑐𝑜𝑚𝑏𝑖𝑛𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛𝑠, 𝑛𝑜𝑡 𝑗𝑢𝑠𝑡 𝑒𝑥𝑝𝑒𝑟𝑖𝑒𝑛𝑐𝑒.

What do you think -
 𝑰𝒔 𝒉𝒊𝒓𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝒂𝒄𝒕𝒖𝒂𝒍𝒍𝒚 𝒊𝒏𝒄𝒓𝒆𝒂𝒔𝒊𝒏𝒈, 𝒐𝒓 𝒂𝒓𝒆 𝒄𝒐𝒎𝒑𝒂𝒏𝒊𝒆𝒔 𝒋𝒖𝒔𝒕 𝒃𝒆𝒄𝒐𝒎𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝒎𝒐𝒓𝒆 𝒔𝒆𝒍𝒆𝒄𝒕𝒊𝒗𝒆?
We would appreciate your thoughts on the comments.

🚀 We’re Hiring: Senior MERN Developer (NestJS & Next.js)

📍 Location: Lahore (Onsite)
🕓 Timings: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM
🧠 Experience Required: 4–6 Years

We are looking for a highly skilled Senior MERN Developer with strong hands-on expertise in NestJS (Backend Architecture) and Next.js (SSR/SSG Applications) to join our growing team in Lahore.

If you have solid experience building scalable backend systems with NestJS and developing high-performance applications using Next.js, we would love to connect with you.

🔹 What We’re Looking For:

✔ Strong command over NestJS (Modules, Guards, Interceptors, Middleware)
✔ Advanced expertise in Next.js (SSR, SSG, Routing, Optimization)
✔ MongoDB database design & performance optimization
✔ RESTful APIs & microservices architecture
✔ TypeScript proficiency

post.text:
🚨 Hiring: Technical Director – MERN Stack | AI-First SaaS | Remote (India | Fully WFH)

Most companies are talking about AI.
We are operationalizing it — across architecture, engineering workflows, testing, and DevOps.

We are a Command Center Solution specialist building an AI-native, multi-tenant SaaS platform powered by Google Maps APIs — and we’re hiring a hands-on Technical Director to lead architecture, scale engineering systems, and embed AI into the DNA of how software gets built.

This is not a coordination role.
This is for a builder-leader who still ships production code.

🔧 What You’ll Own

• Technical direction for MERN stack architecture
• Multi-tenant SaaS scalability & performance engineering
• AI-first engineering workflows (Copilot, AI testing, DevOps automation)
• Mentorship & technical leadership of 5 full-stack engineers
• Architecture decisions tied to product and revenue impact
• Engineering standards, velocity, and platform evolution

✅ What Makes You a Fit

• 5+ years full-stack experience + 2+ years in architecture/technical leadership (Total 7–10 yrs)
• Proven track record scaling MERN applications in production
• Real-world adoption of AI-assisted development tools
• Experience designing multi-tenant SaaS systems
• Strong DevOps & CI/CD implementation exposure
• Complex API integrations (Google Maps preferred)
• Ability to lead distributed teams with ownership & clarity

💰 Compensation & Rewards

• Competitive Fixed CTC: ₹35–55 LPA
• Up to 30% performance-linked annual bonus based on:
– Engineering velocity gains
– Platform scalability & reliability metrics
– AI workflow implementation outcomes
– Team delivery performance
• Real architectural authority & decision ownership
• Remote-first, outcome-driven culture

This is a leadership role where impact directly drives reward.

🌟 Why This Role Stands Apart

• AI is embedded across coding, testing, and deployment — not treated as an add-on
• You shape engineering culture, not just delivery pipelines
• Autonomy in architecture decisions & tooling strategy
• Work in a high-trust, async, deeply technical environment

If you’ve moved beyond experimenting with AI tools and are systematically using AI to multiply engineering leverage — you’ll thrive here.

📩 How to Apply

Please share:
• Resume
• GitHub / Portfolio
• A short note on how AI has transformed your engineering workflow

Bonus: Tell us about the most impactful architectural decision you’ve made in a SaaS system.

📧 Email: mahesh@allcadservices.com

If you're ready to build intelligently scalable platforms — and be rewarded for measurable technical impact — let’s connect.

post.text:
🚀 Hiring: Technical Director – MERN Stack | AI-First SaaS | Remote- Fully WFH (India)
Most companies are “exploring AI.”
 We’re building an AI-native engineering culture.
We’re “A Command Center Solution specialist” hiring a hands-on Technical Director to architect and scale a multi-tenant SaaS platform built on Google Maps APIs — while embedding AI into every stage of development.
This is not a people-manager-only role.
 This is for a leader who still loves writing code.
 
🔧 What You’ll Own
Technical direction for MERN stack architecture
Multi-tenant SaaS scalability
AI-first workflows (Copilot, AI-powered testing, intelligent DevOps automation)
Mentorship of 5 full-stack engineers
Architecture decisions aligned with business impact
Engineering standards, velocity, and long-term system evolution
 

✅ What Makes You a Fit
5+ years full-stack experience, with 2+ years in architecture or technical leadership (Total exp. 7-10yrs)
Proven experience scaling MERN applications
Real-world usage of AI-assisted development tools
Experience designing multi-tenant SaaS systems
Strong DevOps and CI/CD exposure
Experience integrating complex APIs (Google Maps preferred)
Ability to lead distributed teams with clarity and accountability
 

💰 Compensation & Rewards
Competitive fixed CTC: ₹35–55 LPA (based on depth of architecture and SaaS scale experience)
Up to 30% performance-based annual bonus tied to:
Engineering velocity improvements
Platform scalability & reliability metrics
Successful AI workflow implementation
Team performance & delivery outcomes
High ownership and real architectural authority
Remote-first, results-driven culture
This is a leadership role with measurable upside. The stronger the impact, the stronger the reward.
 

🌟 Why This Role Is Different
AI is not a buzzword here — it’s embedded in how we code, test, and deploy.
You will shape engineering culture, not just execute tickets.
You’ll have autonomy in architecture decisions and tool adoption.
You’ll work with a team that values async communication, trust, and technical depth.
If you’ve moved beyond “trying AI tools” and into systematically increasing engineering leverage with AI, this will feel like home.
 

📩 How to Apply
Apply with:
Resume
GitHub or portfolio
A short note on how AI has changed your engineering workflow
Bonus: Tell us the most impactful architectural decision you've made in a SaaS system.

📧 Email: mahesh@allcadservices.com
If you're ready to build something intelligently scalable — and be rewarded for real impact — let’s talk.
 
 #Hiring #TechnicalDirector #MERNStack #AIEngineering #AISaaS #RemoteJobsIndia #WFH #TechLeadership #SaaSArchitecture #FullStackJobs #EngineeringLeadership #AIHiring #DevOps #GoogleMapsAPI #StartupHiring

post.text:
#Hiring - Full Stack Developer (MERN / Node.js)

Location: Candidates must be based in Tamil Nadu
Employment Type: Full-Time | On-Site
Experience: 1 - 3 Years

How to Apply
Share your updated resume via email to: careers@ysquaretechnology.com

Email Subject Line (Mandatory): Application for Full Stack Developer (MERN / Node.js)

Details to Include in the Email Body:
▪️ Current Location
▪️ Total Years of Experience
▪️ Notice Period Duration & Availability
▪️ Current CTC & Expected CTC

Note: Only email applications sent to the above email ID will be considered. Calls or in-person approaches are not encouraged.

About the Role
We are seeking a passionate & detail-oriented Full Stack Developer to join our enterprise product team. The ideal candidate will have strong fundamentals in Node.js, React.js, & TypeScript, along with experience building scalable applications using microservices architecture.
You will be working on enterprise-grade solutions with modern cloud infrastructure & AI-assisted development tools.

Key Responsibilities
▪️Design, develop, & maintain scalable full-stack enterprise applications
▪️Build RESTful APIs using Node.js & Express.js
▪️Develop responsive & dynamic frontend applications using React.js & TypeScript
▪️Implement clean architecture & microservices-based backend systems
▪️Design & optimize databases using MySQL and MongoDB
▪️Work with ORMs such as Prisma & TypeORM
▪️Collaborate with cross-functional teams (QA, DevOps, Product, UI/UX)
▪️Write clean, maintainable, & well-documented code
▪️Participate in code reviews & follow enterprise coding standards
▪️Utilize AI-powered developer tools to improve productivity

Technical Skills Required
▪️Core Technologies
▪️Node.js
▪️Express.js
▪️React.js
▪️TypeScript
▪️Databases
▪️MySQL
▪️MongoDB
▪️ORM Tools
▪️Prisma
▪️TypeORM
▪️Architecture
▪️Microservices Architecture
▪️REST API Design
▪️Basic knowledge of system design principles
▪️AWS Fundamentals
▪️Amazon S3
▪️Amazon EC2
▪️AWS Lambda
▪️Amazon CloudFront

AI Development Tools (any one)
▪️GitHub Copilot
▪️Cursor
▪️Antigravity (AI-assisted development tools)

Preferred Qualifications
▪️Understanding of authentication & authorization (JWT, OAuth)
▪️Experience with version control systems (Git)
▪️Basic CI/CD knowledge
▪️Familiarity with Docker is a plus
▪️Knowledge of performance optimization techniques

Soft Skills (Important for Enterprise Projects)
▪️Strong problem-solving & analytical thinking
▪️Excellent communication skills
▪️Ability to work in a collaborative team environment
▪️Ownership mindset & accountability
▪️Time management & ability to meet deadlines
▪️Adaptability & willingness to learn new technologies
▪️Attention to detail & commitment to quality

What We Offer
▪️Opportunity to work on enterprise-scale projects
▪️Exposure to cloud-native architecture & modern DevOps practices
▪️Collaborative & growth-oriented work environment
▪️Hands-on experience with AI-assisted development tools
▪️Career growth & mentorship

author.name: Preeti Dhami
author.headline: Content Writer|| Freelancer|| Helping Brands Grow Through Content

post.text:
I think recruiters are scared of me.

No, seriously.

Whenever I apply for a job, the hiring manager is usually very happy to connect. They ask for my resume and, in most cases, an assignment (often unpaid). I submit everything on time, sometimes even go through an interview… and then....silence. 

No update.
No feedback.
Not even a small “We’re moving ahead with another candidate” email.

And honestly, I’m not someone who’s going to get angry or start an argument. A simple email would do the job.

I promise I won’t do anything dramatic 😜

But on a serious note, basic communication should be a normal part of the hiring process. We invest time and effort, especially when assignments are involved.

The least we deserve is a simple update.

So, if you’re a recruiter reading this, please don’t ghost candidates.

P.S. Not everyone is like that. Most do reply, it's just the few who ghost that stay in our heads the longest. 😄

#HiringProces #Recruitment #JobSearch #ContentWritingJobs #JustKidding

- Install Nodemailer and React icons using `npm install nodemailer` and `npm install react-icons --save`
- Create an API route to handle email logic from the server side
- Set up a secure API route in Next.js to send emails

Here's what you need to do:
- Create a `.env.local` file with your Gmail credentials
- Use the `nodemailer.createTransport` function to connect to Gmail's SMTP server
- Handle success and error responses to notify the user if their email was sent or not

- Node.js installed

author.name: Prudhvi Shankar
author.headline: System Engineer at Cloud Infrastructure @ Tata Consultancy Services | Technical Support, Cloud Solutions

post.text:
Here is how my job search is going on.

During my job search, I have applied to many companies and attended several interviews. In one particular interview, the discussion initially went well. The interviewer asked questions related to my previous work and experience, and I was able to answer them comfortably.
However, towards the end of the interview, the interviewer asked a question related to Unix. I was surprised because my resume does not mention any experience with Unix, and it was also not part of the job description. After 2 month I got a declined email with no proper information why I got declined.
In many MNCs, especially for entry-level or mid-level roles, employees are often restricted to a specific set of responsibilities and are not usually exposed to multiple roles. If hiring teams expect candidates to have knowledge beyond the listed requirements, it would be helpful to give them an opportunity to learn or express their interest in those areas. This approach can help employees upgrade their skills while also supporting the growth and flexibility of the team.

author.name: Meval Purohit
author.headline: Undergraduate Computer Science Student|MERN|AI/ML

post.text:
Day 10 | Implementing Email Functionality with Nodemailer

Today I explored how backend systems send automated emails to users.

In many real-world applications, emails are essential for things like account verification, password resets, notifications, and onboarding messages. To understand this better, I implemented email functionality using Nodemailer in my Node.js backend.

Instead of using a Gmail password directly, the authentication was handled using Google OAuth 2.0 configured through Google Cloud Console. This approach allows the server to send emails securely without exposing account credentials.

⚙️ What I Implemented

• Integrated Nodemailer to send emails from the backend
• Configured Google OAuth 2.0 credentials using Google Cloud Console
• Generated secure access tokens for sending emails
• Implemented automated email flow triggered by backend events

🧠 Key Learning

Email systems are not just about sending messages. They require secure authentication, proper backend triggers, and reliable delivery mechanisms.

Understanding how services like Nodemailer + OAuth 2.0 work together gave me a clearer picture of how production systems handle user communication.

#BackendDevelopment #NodeJS #Nodemailer #OAuth #BuildInPublic #DeveloperJourney

author.name: Arpan Bera
author.headline: Backend developer with expertise in Node.js & Django framework. Proficient in HTML, CSS, JavaScript & WordPress, with experience building scalable and responsive web applications.

post.text:
🔐 Building a Secure User Signup Flow (Node.js + Express)

While working on an authentication system, I implemented a secure signup controller with email verification using OTP.

Here’s what the flow does:

✔️ Normalizes user input (trim + lowercase for username and email)
✔️ Checks if username or email already exists
✔️ Hashes password securely using bcrypt
✔️ Generates a one-time OTP for email verification
✔️ Stores OTP with an expiry time (10 minutes)
✔️ Sends verification email to the user
✔️ Automatically removes the user if the email fails to send
✔️ Generates a verification JWT token

This ensures that only verified users can activate their accounts and improves the security of the authentication system.

Tech Stack:
Node.js | Express.js | MongoDB | JWT | bcrypt | Email Verification

Currently exploring more ways to build production-ready authentication systems 🚀

#nodejs #expressjs #backenddevelopment #jwt #authentication #webdevelopment #mongodb #developers

post.text:
The most expensive thing in a job search isn't the commute—it’s the hope.
We see the posts every day: 

“We’re hiring! Send your CV to [email].” We spend the time. We polish the bullets. We hit send.

The result?
No acknowledgment.
No "thank you."
No "we’ve moved on."

Ghosting isn't a "busy" problem; it's a culture problem. If you have the platform to ask for someone’s time, you should have the decency to respect it.
Job seekers aren't data points. We’re people. Let’s start acting like it.

author.name: VIPIN SACHDEVA
author.headline: MERN Stack Developer | 1.5 Years Experience | Expert in MongoDB, Express, React & Node.js | Building Full-Stack Web Applications

post.text:
Day 4 of 21 | Middleware is the most underrated concept in backend dev.

Before today, I kept copy-pasting the same console.log() in every route.
Then I discovered middleware and felt like an idiot (in the best way).

𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝗠𝗶𝗱𝗱𝗹𝗲𝘄𝗮𝗿𝗲?
A function that runs BETWEEN the request and the response.

request → [middleware] → [middleware] → response

𝗥𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗲𝘅𝗮𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗲:

const logger = (req, res, next) => {
 console.log(\`\${req.method} \${req.url}\`);
 next(); // 👈 MUST call this or request hangs forever
};

app.use(logger);

𝗧𝘆𝗽𝗲𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗠𝗶𝗱𝗱𝗹𝗲𝘄𝗮𝗿𝗲:
→ Application-level (app.use)
→ Route-level (specific routes only)
→ Error-handling (4 parameters: err, req, res, next)
→ Third-party (cors, morgan, helmet)

𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗯𝗹𝗲𝗺 𝗜 𝗳𝗮𝗰𝗲𝗱:
→ Forgot to call next() - request just hung with no response
→ Spent 20 mins debugging before finding that 😅

𝗚𝗼𝗹𝗱𝗲𝗻 𝗿𝘂𝗹𝗲:
Every middleware MUST either call next() or send a response. No exceptions.

#MERNStack #BackendDevelopment #FullStackDeveloper #NodeJS #ExpressJS #WebDevelopment #SheriyansCodingSchool

author.headline: 3,036 followers

post.text:
𝐍𝐨𝐝𝐞.𝐣𝐬 𝐯𝐬 𝐉𝐚𝐯𝐚 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐄𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐩𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐞 𝐀𝐩𝐩𝐬 — 𝐖𝐡𝐢𝐜𝐡 𝐒𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐥𝐝 𝐘𝐨𝐮 𝐂𝐡𝐨𝐨𝐬𝐞 𝐢𝐧 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓?

After working across both stacks in production environments, here's my honest breakdown:

𝐍𝐨𝐝𝐞.𝐣𝐬 𝐖𝐢𝐧𝐬 𝐖𝐡𝐞𝐧: → You need real-time features (chat, live dashboards, streaming) → Your team is full-stack JavaScript → Microservices & API-first architecture is your game → Speed to market matters more than rigid structure → You're building I/O-heavy, event-driven systems

𝐉𝐚𝐯𝐚 𝐖𝐢𝐧𝐬 𝐖𝐡𝐞𝐧: → You're operating at serious enterprise scale (think banking, healthcare, telecom) → Strong typing, mature tooling & long-term maintainability are non-negotiable → You need battle-tested frameworks like Spring Boot → Compliance, security, and auditability are core requirements → Your team size is large and code consistency is critical

𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐡𝐨𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐭𝐡?

Most teams pick the wrong stack for the wrong reasons — chasing hype instead of matching the tool to the problem.

𝐍𝐨𝐝𝐞 𝐢𝐬 𝐛𝐫𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐚𝐧𝐭 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐞𝐝 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐟𝐥𝐞𝐱𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲. 𝐉𝐚𝐯𝐚 𝐢𝐬 𝐮𝐧𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐝 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐬𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐞.

Neither is universally "better." The best engineers I've worked with ask "what does this system actually need?" before picking a stack.

My rule of thumb: → Startup or product team? Node.js. → Large org, complex domain, long-lived codebase? Java. → Both in your org? That's what APIs are for. 

𝐖𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐜𝐤 𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐫𝐮𝐧𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐢𝐧 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐫𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐭 𝐧𝐨𝐰 — 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐰𝐨𝐮𝐥𝐝 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐜𝐡𝐨𝐨𝐬𝐞 𝐢𝐭 𝐚𝐠𝐚𝐢𝐧?
👇 𝐃𝐫𝐨𝐩 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐡𝐨𝐭 𝐭𝐚𝐤𝐞 𝐛𝐞𝐥𝐨𝐰.

🚀 Building my backend foundation by learning Node.js step by step
While exploring backend development, I realized that using frameworks is easier when the core concepts are clear.

So I started spending time understanding how Node.js works internally instead of directly depending on frameworks.

Here are some of the topics I’ve been exploring:
 ✅ Understanding Node.js
 ✔ How JavaScript can run on the server using Node.js
 ✔ Role of the V8 engine in executing JavaScript
 ✔ How server-side JavaScript differs from browser JavaScript
 
✅ How Node.js Works
 ✔ Event-driven programming model
 ✔ Non-blocking operations for better performance
 ✔ Handling many requests without slowing down the server
 
✅ Working with Modules
 ✔ Understanding the CommonJS module system
 ✔ Importing files using require()
 ✔ Exporting functions and variables using module.exports

💡 Key takeaway:
 Learning the basics helps in understanding what happens behind the scenes in backend applications.

📚 Next things I plan to learn:
 ✔ Event Loop and how asynchronous tasks are handled
 ✔ Streams and Buffers in Node.js
 ✔ Creating REST APIs using Express.js
 
Learning step by step and focusing on fundamentals.
Excited to continue improving my backend development skills!

post.text:
A realistic full-stack web development roadmap:

Frontend
→ Learn HTML (structure)
→ Learn CSS (Flexbox, Grid, responsive design)
→ Learn JavaScript properly (DOM, async, fetch API)
→ Pick a framework: React / Vue.js / Angular
→ Styling system: Tailwind CSS / Bootstrap
→ Learn Next.js (routing, SSR, APIs)

Backend
→ Learn Node.js
→ Backend framework: Express.js or NestJS
→ Learn REST APIs
→ Authentication (JWT, sessions, OAuth)

Database
→ Learn SQL basics
→ Use PostgreSQL or MySQL
→ Optional NoSQL: MongoDB
→ ORM: Prisma or Mongoose

Frontend ↔ Backend integration
→ HTTP requests (fetch / Axios)
→ API design
→ State management: Redux / Zustand

Dev tools
→ Version control: Git
→ Hosting: Vercel, Netlify, Render
→ Containers: Docker
→ CI/CD basics

Deployment
→ Deploy frontend
→ Deploy backend APIs
→ Connect domain & environment variables

The real step
Ship projects.
Break things.
Debug production errors.

Congrats you’re on the path to becoming a full-stack developer.

author.name: Aryan Agrawal
author.headline: MERN Stack Developer | Building Projects in React, Node.js

post.text:
Yesterday I built a Gym Website using the MERN stack for practice, but the main thing I wanted to focus on in this project was practicing frontend, backend and APIs actually working together in a real application.

So I kept the frontend simple.

I built the frontend in around 1 hour using React and Tailwind CSS, because the main thing I wanted to focus on was the backend logic and API communication rather than spending too much time on UI design.

The website also includes a BMI calculator (i made it for the first time), where users can enter their height and weight to calculate their BMI instantly on the frontend.

The stack I used in this project:

Frontend
React + Vite
Tailwind CSS
Axios
React Toastify for notifications
React Spinners for loading state

Backend
Node.js
Express.js
Nodemailer for sending emails
Dotenv for environment variables
CORS handling

The most interesting part of this project is the Contact section. (the part i really made this project for)

A user enters:

Name
Email
Message

When the form is submitted:

User fills the contact form
React sends the data to the backend using Axios
Express receives the request
Nodemailer sends the message directly to the admin email inbox

So what looks like a simple contact form on a website actually becomes a real working communication system between the website user and the website owner.

In the video I’m uploading I’ll show a quick glance of the frontend including the BMI calculator, and then demonstrate the main feature where filling the contact form triggers a real email to the admin inbox.

Projects like this are helping me understand how frontend, backend, APIs and external services connect together in real web applications. Checkout my github for the code the link is in the profile. 

Should i now make it responsive or go on to the next project? What do you suggest?? 😋 

#MERN #WebDevelopment #ReactJS #NodeJS #FullStackDevelopment #LearningInPublic

author.name: Taofique Islam
author.headline: Frontend & Backend Engineer | JavaScript, Node.js, APIs, Scalable Web Applications

post.text:
🔐 What I learned building a production-ready auth system from scratch (MERN Stack)

Just wrapped up a full authentication system and wanted to share some lessons that genuinely shifted how I think about backend architecture.
The interesting parts:

🔹 JWT isn't just about signing tokens — how you design the payload and where you store it (HTTP-only cookies vs localStorage) has real security implications
🔹 Middleware-driven identity flow changed how I see request lifecycles. Decode token → attach userId to request → controller consumes it. Clean, stateless, scalable.
🔹 Email workflows are deceptively complex. Verification tokens, expiry windows, reset links — each one is a small security surface you have to think through carefully.
🔹 Switched from Mailtrap to Resend mid-project. Mailtrap's demo domain has a hard 20-email limit which is fine for initial testing but hits fast. Resend gave me 100 emails/day on the free tier, actual inbox delivery, and a much cleaner API. Worth the switch early.

Stack: MongoDB • Express • React • Node.js • Resend

The biggest mindset shift: authentication isn't a feature you bolt on — it's infrastructure you design around from day one.
Currently documenting the full flow for a walkthrough. If you're working on similar backend security concepts, let's connect 🤝

#MERNStack #BackendDevelopment #WebSecurity #NodeJS #FullStackDevelopment

author.name: jaya Surya
author.headline: Front-end Engineer (React/Next.js) | TypeScript | Building Scalable, High-Performance Web Applications

post.text:
Why I chose Resend for my latest portfolio project 🚀

Sending emails is a core requirement for almost every web app, but your choice depends entirely on your architecture. After experimenting with the top three options, here is the breakdown:

🔹Nodemailer (The Industry Standard)

A Node.js library for SMTP. It’s the "DIY" approach to email.

Best for: Custom backend servers (Express/Fastify) where you want total control.
Pro: Completely free (if you have an SMTP server) and zero vendor lock-in.
Con: Setting up and maintaining your own SMTP server can be a headache; deliverability can suffer if not configured correctly.

🔹Resend (The Modern Favorite)

The "Stripe for emails." A modern API built for developers who want speed and DX.

Best for: Next.js / Serverless environments.
Pro: Incredible Developer Experience (DX), native React Email support, and fast delivery.
Con: Requires domain verification (not ideal for "quick-and-dirty" prototyping).

🔹EmailJS (The Frontend Shortcut)

Allows you to send emails directly from the browser. No backend? No problem.

Best for: Static sites and simple contact forms where you don't have a backend.
Pro: Zero server setup—it’s essentially "plug-and-play."
Con: Exposes your service limits to the frontend; less secure for sensitive applications; limited free tier.

📌 My Verdict

I used Resend for my own portfolio. It integrated seamlessly with my Next.js setup, and the ability to build templates with React components was a game-changer.

Each tool has its own use case depending on your application architecture.

Have you used any of these tools in your projects? Which one do you prefer? 🤔
Let’s discuss in the comments! 👇

#webdev #coding #javascript #programming #nextjs #resend #softwareengineering

author.name: Sohel Khan
author.headline: Founder & CEO of RoadmapFinder | AI-Powered Learning Roadmaps + Best Resources | Helping Students Go From Confused to Job-Ready Faster.

post.text:
Most developers learn JavaScript.
But stop at the frontend.
If you want to build real full-stack applications, learn Node.js.
Here’s the Node.js Roadmap from beginner → production-ready:

Phase 1 — JavaScript Fundamentals
Before Node.js, master:
• ES6+ syntax
• Async / Await
• Promises
• Event loop basics
• Modules
Node is JavaScript on the server.

Phase 2 — Node.js Core
Learn how Node actually works:
• Node architecture
• File system module
• Path & OS modules
• HTTP module
• NPM ecosystem
Now you understand the runtime.

Phase 3 — Express.js
This is where real backend development begins.
• REST APIs
• Routing
• Middleware
• Request / Response handling
• Error handling
Express powers most Node backends.

Phase 4 — Database Integration
Backend needs data.
Learn:
• MongoDB / PostgreSQL
• Mongoose ORM
• CRUD operations
• Data modeling

Phase 5 — Authentication & Security
• JWT authentication
• OAuth basics
• Password hashing
• API security
Now your apps are production-safe.

Phase 6 — Production & Deployment
• Environment variables
• Logging
• Docker basics
• Deployment (AWS / Render / Vercel)
Shipping matters more than tutorials.
Node.js is still one of the most demanded backend skills.

📌 Full roadmap + best learning resources in comments
💬 Comment NODE if you're learning backend

#Nodejs #BackendDevelopment #WebDevelopment #JavaScript #Programming

🚀 Before jumping into frameworks like Express, I decided to first understand how Node.js actually works.

While strengthening my backend development skills, I started focusing on the core fundamentals of Node.js instead of directly relying on frameworks.

Here are some of the concepts I’ve been learning:

🔹 Node.js Fundamentals
 ✔ How Node.js runs JavaScript outside the browser
 ✔ Understanding the V8 JavaScript Engine
 ✔ Difference between browser runtime and server runtime

🔹 Core Architecture
 ✔ Event-driven architecture
 ✔ Non-blocking I/O model
 ✔ How Node.js handles multiple requests efficiently

🔹 Modules & Code Organization
 ✔ Common JS module system
 ✔ Using require() and module. exports
 ✔ Structuring applications across multiple files

Understanding these concepts gives better clarity on how backend applications interact with the system and manage asynchronous operations.

Next concepts on my learning roadmap:

 ✔ Event Loop phases
 ✔ Streams & Buffers
 ✔ Building REST APIs using Express.js

Because in the long run, frameworks may change — but strong fundamentals will always stay relevant. 💡

Save this guide for quick access while coding. Here are some key points:
- Check Node and NPM version: node -v
- Initialize a Node project: npm init
- Import and export modules using the CommonJS module system
- Use the fs module to work with files:
 • Read a file: fs.readFile
 • Write a file: fs.writeFile
 • Append to a file: fs.appendFile
- Create a simple HTTP server using the http module
- Install packages with NPM:
 • Install a package: npm install express
 • Install globally: npm install -g nodemon
 • Remove package: npm uninstall package-name
- Use async/await for asynchronous code
- Useful tools: Express.js, Nodemon, Docker, Git

author.headline: Technical Trainer & Software Developer @ CipherSchools | Ex- Bhashini (Ministry of Electronics & IT, Govt. of India) | 3⭐ CodeChef

post.text:
Doing the same thing again and again with the same set of keys is… boring.

Every day I 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝘁𝗼 𝗪𝗶𝗙𝗶, opened the browser, went to the login page, typed the username, typed the password, checked the terms box, and clicked login.

𝗡𝗼𝘁 𝗱𝗶𝗳𝗳𝗶𝗰𝘂𝗹𝘁.
𝗝𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝗿𝗲𝗽𝗲𝘁𝗶𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲.

And repetition is exactly where 𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗼𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀.

So instead of pressing the same keys every time, 𝗜 𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝘀𝗼𝗺𝗲 𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮 𝘀𝗺𝗮𝗹𝗹 𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗼𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝘀𝗰𝗿𝗶𝗽𝘁.

Now with 𝗼𝗻𝗲 𝗰𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗸, the script:

• Opens the WiFi login portal
• Detects if I'm already connected
• If not, fills the username and password
• Checks the terms checkbox
• Submits the form
• Confirms login success
• Closes the browser

I even added support for 𝗺𝘂𝗹𝘁𝗶𝗽𝗹𝗲 𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗮𝗹𝘀, so I can choose which account to use when connecting.

Under the hood it uses 𝗣𝗹𝗮𝘆𝘄𝗿𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝗡𝗼𝗱𝗲.𝗷𝘀, controlling Microsoft Edge to interact with the page just like a real user would.

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗽𝗮𝗿𝘁?
What used to take multiple steps now takes one click.

This wasn’t a big project.
But it’s a reminder of 𝘀𝗼𝗺𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗺𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗮𝗻𝘁:

If you find yourself repeating the same steps every day, that’s usually a sign that 𝗶𝘁 𝗰𝗮𝗻 𝗯𝗲 𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗼𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗱.

Small automations like this save time, reduce friction, and make everyday workflows smoother.

𝘚𝘰𝘮𝘦𝘵𝘪𝘮𝘦𝘴 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘣𝘦𝘴𝘵 𝘱𝘳𝘰𝘥𝘶𝘤𝘵𝘪𝘷𝘪𝘵𝘺 𝘶𝘱𝘨𝘳𝘢𝘥𝘦 𝘪𝘴𝘯’𝘵 𝘸𝘰𝘳𝘬𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘧𝘢𝘴𝘵𝘦𝘳 -
𝘪𝘵’𝘴 𝙧𝙚𝙢𝙤𝙫𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙬𝙤𝙧𝙠 𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙞𝙧𝙚𝙡𝙮.

author.name: Luv Tomar
author.headline: Software Engineering Student | JavaScript, React & Node.js | Backend & DSA | 2027 Graduate

post.text:
Working on the backend of a developer networking platform I'm building called DevBridge.
Today I implemented some core backend APIs using Node.js, Express, and MongoDB (Mongoose).
APIs built so far:
• POST /signup → create a new user
• GET /user → fetch user by email
• GET /feed → get all users
• PATCH /user → update user details
• DELETE /user → delete a user
While building these APIs I understood a few important things about working with Mongoose and Express.
Some useful things I learned:
• User.find({}) returns all documents from the collection
• User.find({ emailId: value }) returns documents matching the condition
• findByIdAndDelete(id) deletes a document directly using the MongoDB _id
Also a small but important middleware in Express:
app.use(express.json())
Without this middleware, the server cannot read JSON data from the request body.
This middleware parses incoming JSON and makes it accessible through req.body.
Tested the APIs using Postman (screenshot below).
Next steps for DevBridge:
• Input validation
• Password encryption
• Authentication (JWT)
• Building the React frontend
Trying to focus on understanding how the backend actually works instead of just wiring things together.

#NodeJS #BackendDevelopment #MongoDB #ExpressJS #FullStackDevelopment

author.headline: Founder & CEO of RoadmapFinder | AI-Powered Learning Roadmaps + Best Resources | Helping Students Go From Confused to Job-Ready Faster.

post.text:
If you know JavaScript…
You’re one step away from backend development. Learn Express.js.
Here’s a clear Express.js roadmap from beginner → production-ready.

Phase 1 — JavaScript + Node Basics
Before Express, understand:
• ES6+ syntax
• Async / Await
• Promises
• Node.js modules
• Event loop basics
Express runs on Node.js.

Phase 2 — Express Fundamentals
Start building servers:
• Express setup
• Routing
• Request / Response objects
• Middleware basics
• Static files
Now you can build simple APIs.

Phase 3 — REST API Development
This is the real backend skill.
• CRUD operations
• Route structure
• API versioning
• Validation
• Error handling
Now your APIs become production-ready.

Phase 4 — Database Integration
Backend = data.
Learn to connect:
• MongoDB
• PostgreSQL
• ORM / ODM tools
• Data modeling

Phase 5 — Authentication & Security
• JWT authentication
• Password hashing
• Role-based access
• API security best practices
Now your backend is secure.

Phase 6 — Production & Deployment
• Environment variables
• Logging
• Rate limiting
• Deployment (AWS / Render / Vercel)

Shipping matters more than learning.
Mastering Express.js means you can build real backend services with JavaScript.
